name: Process Engineering Ontology (PE-ONT)
description: Comprehensive ontology for defining, orchestrating, and optimizing business processes with agentic AI augmentation. Supports cradle-to-grave process management from vision through execution and continuous improvement. v4.0.0 adds formal Skill and Plugin extensibility, and ProcessPath directed-graph composition for flexible cross-process and cross-series routing.
oaa:domain: Business Process Management, Process Engineering, Agentic Orchestration, Skill Composition, Plugin Extensibility

creator.name: Platform Foundation Core - Ontology Architect Agent

oaa:joinPatterns.name: Skill Invocation Chain
oaa:joinPatterns.description: Trace which skills a process phase invokes, and which agents or plugins provide those skills
oaa:joinPatterns.joinPath: pe:ProcessPhase → invokesSkill → pe:Skill ← agentProvidesSkill ← pe:AIAgent; pe:Skill ← providesSkill ← pe:Plugin
oaa:joinPatterns.useCase: Discover all available skill providers for a given phase's skill requirements

oaa:joinPatterns.name: Process Path Traversal
oaa:joinPatterns.description: Walk a ProcessPath directed graph from any entry-point step through links to reachable steps
oaa:joinPatterns.joinPath: pe:ProcessPath → hasPathStep → pe:PathStep [entryPoint=true]; pe:ProcessPath → hasPathLink → pe:PathLink → linkFrom/linkTo → pe:PathStep
oaa:joinPatterns.useCase: Enumerate all reachable process routes from a given entry point with binding strengths

oaa:joinPatterns.name: Cross-Series Path Resolution
oaa:joinPatterns.description: Resolve path steps to their owning ontology processes across series boundaries
oaa:joinPatterns.joinPath: pe:PathStep → stepRefersToProcess → pe:Process [ontologyRef identifies series]; pe:PathStep → stepInvokesSkill → pe:Skill [owningOntology identifies series]
oaa:joinPatterns.useCase: Map a cross-series path (e.g. PPM→EFS→VSOM→OKR→KPI→VP) to concrete process definitions in each series

oaa:joinPatterns.name: Plugin Dependency Resolution
oaa:joinPatterns.description: Resolve the full dependency tree of a plugin including transitive plugin requirements and their provided skills
oaa:joinPatterns.joinPath: pe:Plugin → pluginRequires → pe:Plugin (recursive); pe:Plugin → providesSkill → pe:Skill
oaa:joinPatterns.useCase: Determine the complete set of skills available when a plugin and all its dependencies are installed

oaa:joinPatterns.name: Skill Dependency Chain
oaa:joinPatterns.description: Trace transitive skill dependencies to determine execution order
oaa:joinPatterns.useCase: Build the execution DAG for a skill invocation, resolving all prerequisite skills

oaa:joinPatterns.name: Instance-Scoped Path Discovery
oaa:joinPatterns.description: Find all process paths available for a given PFI instance configuration
oaa:joinPatterns.useCase: Present instance-specific process routes alongside universal cross-series paths

oaa:joinPatterns.name: Design System Plugin Bridge
oaa:joinPatterns.description: Connect design-system type plugins to their target DesignSystem entities
oaa:joinPatterns.useCase: Discover which plugins extend a given design system and what skills they provide

oaa:joinPatterns.name: Phase-to-Value-Chain Lineage
oaa:joinPatterns.description: Trace a process phase through its parent process to the value chain, then through path compositions to downstream processes
oaa:joinPatterns.joinPath: pe:ProcessPhase ← hasPhase ← pe:Process → partOfValueChain → pe:ValueChain; pe:Process ← stepRefersToProcess ← pe:PathStep ← linkFrom ← pe:PathLink → linkTo → pe:PathStep → stepRefersToProcess → pe:Process
oaa:joinPatterns.useCase: Full process lineage: from a phase, up to its process, across to value chain siblings, and through path links to downstream processes

entities.name: Process
entities.description: A structured, repeatable sequence of activities and decision points that transforms inputs into outputs to achieve specific business outcomes, optimized through AI augmentation.

oaa:properties.name: businessObjective
oaa:properties.description: Strategic business objective this process supports

oaa:properties.name: scope
oaa:properties.description: Boundaries and coverage of the process

oaa:properties.name: automationLevel
oaa:properties.description: AI augmentation percentage (0-100)

entities.name: ProcessPhase
entities.description: A distinct stage within a process that groups related activities, has specific deliverables, and includes quality gates for progression control.

oaa:properties.name: phaseNumber
oaa:properties.description: Sequential position in process

oaa:properties.name: entryConditions
oaa:properties.description: Prerequisites before phase can begin

oaa:properties.name: exitConditions
oaa:properties.description: Criteria to complete phase

oaa:properties.name: activities
oaa:properties.description: List of activities in this phase

entities.name: ProcessArtifact
entities.description: Tangible output or deliverable produced by a process phase, serving as evidence of completion and input to downstream activities.

entities.name: ProcessGate
entities.description: Decision point or quality checkpoint that controls progression between process phases, enforcing quality standards and completion criteria.

oaa:properties.name: criteria
oaa:properties.description: Criteria to pass the gate

oaa:properties.name: threshold
oaa:properties.description: Percentage threshold (0-100)

entities.name: ProcessMetric
entities.description: Measurable indicator that tracks process performance, efficiency, quality, or business outcome achievement.

oaa:properties.name: formula
oaa:properties.description: Calculation formula

oaa:properties.name: frequency
oaa:properties.description: Measurement frequency

entities.name: AIAgent
entities.description: Artificial intelligence agent that augments or automates process activities, with defined capabilities, autonomy levels, and coordination protocols. In v4.0.0, capabilities are formalised via the agentProvidesSkill relationship to Skill entities.

oaa:properties.name: capabilities
oaa:properties.description: Legacy free-text capabilities list. Prefer agentProvidesSkill relationship to formal Skill entities.
oaa:properties.oaa:deprecationNote: Use pe:agentProvidesSkill relationship to pe:Skill entities instead. Retained for backward compatibility.

oaa:properties.name: model
oaa:properties.description: Underlying AI model

oaa:properties.name: qualityThreshold
oaa:properties.description: Minimum quality score (0-100)

entities.name: ProcessInstance
entities.description: Specific execution of a process definition, tracking actual progress, performance, and outcomes for a particular initiative.

oaa:properties.name: currentPhase
oaa:properties.description: Currently executing phase

oaa:properties.name: progress
oaa:properties.description: Completion percentage (0-100)

entities.name: Hypothesis
entities.description: Testable assumption about process improvement or outcome prediction, validated through systematic measurement during process execution.

oaa:properties.name: statement
oaa:properties.description: Clear hypothesis statement

oaa:properties.name: measurementMethod
oaa:properties.description: How to validate

oaa:properties.name: target
oaa:properties.description: Target outcome

oaa:properties.name: confidence
oaa:properties.description: Confidence level (0-100)

entities.name: ValueChain
entities.description: Sequence of value-adding activities that transform inputs through processes to create customer value and business outcomes.

oaa:properties.name: customerValue
oaa:properties.description: Value delivered to customers

oaa:properties.name: inputs
oaa:properties.description: Required inputs

oaa:properties.name: outputs
oaa:properties.description: Outputs delivered

entities.name: ProcessPattern
entities.description: Reusable process template or best practice pattern that can be adapted across different contexts. ProcessPaths may implement ProcessPatterns via pathImplementsPattern.

oaa:properties.name: context
oaa:properties.description: Applicability context

oaa:properties.name: problem
oaa:properties.description: Problem addressed

oaa:properties.name: solution
oaa:properties.description: Solution approach

oaa:properties.name: benefits
oaa:properties.description: Key benefits

entities.name: Skill
entities.description: A typed, invokable capability with formal inputs and outputs. Skills are the atomic unit of callable behaviour — any ProcessPhase, AIAgent, or PathStep can invoke a Skill. Skills are cross-cutting: a single Skill (e.g. vsom:analyseVision) can be invoked from processes in any series without entering the full owning process. Skills may depend on other skills via skillDependsOn, and are provided by Plugins or AIAgents.

oaa:properties.name: inputs
oaa:properties.description: JSON array of typed input parameters, each with name, dataType, and required flag. Example: [{"name":"ontologyId","dataType":"string","required":true}]

oaa:properties.name: outputs
oaa:properties.description: JSON array of typed output parameters, each with name and dataType. Example: [{"name":"analysisResult","dataType":"object"}]

oaa:properties.name: provider
oaa:properties.description: What kind of provider executes this skill

oaa:properties.name: qualityThreshold
oaa:properties.description: Minimum quality score for skill output (0-100)

oaa:properties.name: idempotent
oaa:properties.description: Whether invoking this skill multiple times with same inputs produces same outputs

oaa:properties.name: owningOntology
oaa:properties.description: The ontology that defines the domain of this skill (e.g. VSOM-ONT, KPI-ONT). Enables cross-series skill invocation traceability.

entities.name: Plugin
entities.description: An extension bundle that provides one or more Skills plus configuration. Plugins are the deployment unit for extensibility — a design-system plugin might provide extractTokens, validateContrast, and syncFigma skills. Plugins follow the EMC cascade tier model (PFC/PFI/Product/App) and may depend on other plugins.

oaa:properties.name: configSchema
oaa:properties.description: JSON Schema defining the plugin's configuration surface. Consumers must supply valid configuration matching this schema.

oaa:properties.name: cascadeTier
oaa:properties.description: Cascade tier at which this plugin operates. PFC plugins are platform-wide, PFI plugins are instance-scoped.

oaa:properties.name: entryPoint
oaa:properties.description: Module path or URL for plugin initialisation (e.g. './plugins/ds-figma/index.js')

entities.name: ProcessPath
entities.description: A directed graph of PathSteps connected by PathLinks, defining a reusable, flexible composition of processes and skills across ontology series. ProcessPaths have NO fixed start node — any PathStep marked as entryPoint can be entered directly. The graph encodes what CAN follow, not what MUST start. Binding strength on links (mandatory/recommended/optional/conditional) expresses flexibility. A single PathStep may appear in multiple ProcessPaths with different links. Mirrors EMC-ONT ComposedGraphSpec pattern for process composition.

oaa:properties.name: pathType
oaa:properties.description: Topology hint: linear (simple chain), branching (fork/join), graph (arbitrary directed), pipeline (strict stages)

oaa:properties.name: scope
oaa:properties.description: Whether this path spans ontology series, stays within one series, or is scoped to a PFI instance

oaa:properties.name: seriesRefs
oaa:properties.description: Comma-separated list of ontology series this path spans (e.g. 'PE-Series,VE-Series'). Informational for cross-series paths.

oaa:properties.name: instanceScope
oaa:properties.description: PFI instance name if scope is instance-specific (e.g. 'PFI-BAIV', 'PFI-W4M-WWG')

entities.name: PathStep
entities.description: A node in a ProcessPath directed graph. Each step references either a Process (from any ontology series) or a Skill (for direct invocation without entering a full process). Steps declare whether they can be entered directly (entryPoint) and under what conditions they are available (conditionExpr). A single step can participate in multiple paths with different PathLinks.

oaa:properties.name: stepType
oaa:properties.description: Whether this step must be executed (required), may be skipped (optional), depends on a condition (conditional), or can run concurrently with siblings (parallel)

oaa:properties.name: entryPoint
oaa:properties.description: Whether this step can be entered directly without traversing prior links. At least one step per path must be an entry point.

oaa:properties.name: conditionExpr
oaa:properties.description: State expression determining when this step is available. Uses dot-notation state paths (e.g. 'scope.includes(VE-Series)', 'instance.ontologies.includes(LSC)'). Null means always available.

oaa:properties.name: ontologyRef
oaa:properties.description: Ontology ID that owns the referenced process or skill (e.g. 'VSOM-ONT', 'PPM-ONT', 'KPI-ONT'). Enables cross-series traceability.

entities.name: PathLink
entities.description: A directed edge in a ProcessPath graph connecting two PathSteps. PathLinks carry binding strength (how strongly the transition is recommended) and link type (sequential, parallel, fork, join, fallback). Conditional links activate only when their condition expression evaluates true. Weight enables priority ordering when multiple outbound links exist.

oaa:properties.name: bindingStrength
oaa:properties.description: How strongly this transition is prescribed. Mandatory: must follow if source step completes. Recommended: should follow, but alternatives exist. Optional: may follow. Conditional: follows only if condition is met.

oaa:properties.name: linkType
oaa:properties.description: Transition semantics. Sequential: one after another. Parallel: concurrent execution. Fork: one source to multiple targets. Join: multiple sources converge. Fallback: alternative if primary link fails.

oaa:properties.name: condition
oaa:properties.description: State expression that must evaluate true for this link to activate. Uses same dot-notation as PathStep.conditionExpr. Required when bindingStrength is 'conditional'.

oaa:properties.name: weight
oaa:properties.description: Priority weight when multiple outbound links exist from the same source step. Higher weight = higher priority. Used for path recommendation ordering.

oaa:properties.name: description
oaa:properties.description: Human-readable description of why this transition exists

relationships.name: Has Phase
relationships.description: A process contains one or more ordered phases

relationships.name: Produces
relationships.description: A phase produces artifacts as deliverables

relationships.name: Has Gate
relationships.description: A phase has quality gates controlling progression

relationships.name: Measures
relationships.description: A process tracks metrics for performance

relationships.name: Augmented By
relationships.description: A phase is augmented by AI agents

relationships.name: Depends On
relationships.description: A phase depends on other phases completing first

relationships.name: Instantiates
relationships.description: A process definition creates execution instances

relationships.name: Validates
relationships.description: A process validates hypotheses through measurement

relationships.name: Measured By
relationships.description: A hypothesis is measured by process metrics

relationships.name: Part Of Value Chain
relationships.description: A process is part of a value chain

relationships.name: Includes
relationships.description: A value chain includes processes

relationships.name: Implements Pattern
relationships.description: A process implements reusable patterns

relationships.name: Input To
relationships.description: An artifact serves as input to downstream phases

relationships.name: Coordinates With
relationships.description: An AI agent coordinates with other agents

relationships.name: Invokes Skill
relationships.description: A process phase invokes a skill during execution. Enables cross-cutting skill invocation — a PPM phase can invoke a VSOM skill without entering the full VSOM process.

relationships.name: Agent Provides Skill
relationships.description: An AI agent provides (can execute) a formal skill. Replaces the legacy free-text capabilities property with typed skill bindings.

relationships.name: Skill Depends On
relationships.description: A skill depends on another skill's output. Enables skill chaining (e.g. validateOntology depends on parseOntology).

relationships.name: Provides Skill
relationships.description: A plugin provides one or more skills as its callable surface. Every plugin must provide at least one skill.

relationships.name: Plugin Requires
relationships.description: A plugin depends on another plugin being installed and active. Enables plugin dependency chains.

relationships.name: Plugin Extends Design System
relationships.description: A design-system plugin extends a DesignSystem entity from DS-ONT. Cross-ontology bridge.

relationships.name: Has Path Step
relationships.description: A process path contains path steps as nodes in its directed graph.

relationships.name: Has Path Link
relationships.description: A process path contains path links as directed edges between its steps.

relationships.name: Step Refers To Process
relationships.description: A path step references a process definition from any ontology series. The process is identified by its @id (e.g. ppm:Process, vsom:Process). Cross-ontology by design.

relationships.name: Step Invokes Skill
relationships.description: A path step invokes a skill directly instead of entering a full process. Mutually exclusive with stepRefersToProcess — a step references either a process OR a skill, not both.

relationships.name: Link From
relationships.description: The source path step of a directed link.

relationships.name: Link To
relationships.description: The target path step of a directed link.

relationships.name: Path Implements Pattern
relationships.description: A process path implements a reusable process pattern. Multiple paths may implement the same pattern with different step configurations.

relationships.name: Path Scoped To Instance
relationships.description: A process path is scoped to a specific EMC instance configuration. Enables instance-specific paths (e.g. W4M-WWG has different paths than BAIV).

businessRules.name: Phase Sequence Validation
businessRules.description: Dependency phases must complete before dependent phase begins
businessRules.condition: IF a ProcessPhase has dependsOn relationships
businessRules.action: THEN all dependency phases MUST be completed before this phase can begin

businessRules.name: Gate Enforcement
businessRules.description: Blocking gates must pass for phase completion
businessRules.condition: IF a ProcessPhase has blocking ProcessGates
businessRules.action: THEN all blocking gates MUST pass before phase can complete

businessRules.name: Mandatory Artifact Production
businessRules.description: Mandatory artifacts must be produced before phase completion
businessRules.condition: IF a ProcessArtifact is marked as mandatory
businessRules.action: THEN the producing phase MUST NOT complete until artifact is produced and validated

businessRules.name: Metric Threshold Monitoring
businessRules.condition: IF actual ProcessMetric value falls below threshold
businessRules.action: THEN system MUST generate alert and trigger escalation path

businessRules.name: Agent Autonomy Supervision
businessRules.action: THEN agent outputs MUST require human review before downstream use

businessRules.name: Process Instance Blockers
businessRules.description: Instances with blockers must show blocked status
businessRules.action: THEN status MUST be 'blocked' until blockers are resolved

businessRules.name: Circular Dependency Prevention
businessRules.condition: IF phase A dependsOn phase B AND phase B dependsOn phase A
businessRules.action: THEN configuration MUST be rejected as circular dependency

businessRules.name: Value Chain Completeness
businessRules.description: Value chains must have complete process definitions
businessRules.action: THEN all upstream processes in value chain MUST be defined

businessRules.name: Path Entry Point Required
businessRules.description: Every ProcessPath must have at least one PathStep with entryPoint=true
businessRules.action: THEN at least one of its PathSteps MUST have entryPoint set to true

businessRules.name: No Circular Path Links
businessRules.description: PathLink chains must not form cycles where mandatory binding strength would create infinite loops
businessRules.condition: IF a chain of PathLinks with bindingStrength 'mandatory' forms a cycle (A->B->...->A)
businessRules.action: THEN configuration MUST be rejected. Cycles are permitted only when all links in the cycle have bindingStrength 'optional' or 'conditional'.

businessRules.name: Mandatory Links Must Be Satisfiable
businessRules.description: Mandatory links must connect to steps that can actually be reached
businessRules.action: THEN the target PathStep MUST NOT have a conditionExpr that permanently evaluates false, AND the target step MUST have stepType 'required' or 'conditional' (not 'optional')

businessRules.name: Conditional Link Requires Condition
businessRules.description: PathLinks with bindingStrength 'conditional' must specify a condition expression
businessRules.action: THEN the link MUST have a non-empty condition property

businessRules.name: Step Must Reference Process Or Skill
businessRules.description: Each PathStep must reference either a process or a skill, not both and not neither
businessRules.condition: IF a PathStep exists in a ProcessPath
businessRules.action: THEN the step MUST have exactly one of stepRefersToProcess OR stepInvokesSkill relationships (mutually exclusive, one required)

businessRules.name: Skill Must Have Provider
businessRules.description: Every active skill must be provided by at least one agent or plugin
businessRules.action: THEN the Skill MUST be the target of at least one agentProvidesSkill OR providesSkill relationship

businessRules.name: No Circular Skill Dependencies
businessRules.description: Skill dependency chains must not form cycles
businessRules.condition: IF Skill A skillDependsOn Skill B AND Skill B skillDependsOn Skill A (directly or transitively)
businessRules.action: THEN configuration MUST be rejected as circular skill dependency

businessRules.name: Plugin Must Provide Skills
businessRules.description: Every plugin must expose at least one skill
businessRules.action: THEN the Plugin MUST have at least one providesSkill relationship to a Skill entity

businessRules.name: No Circular Plugin Dependencies
businessRules.description: Plugin dependency chains must not form cycles
businessRules.condition: IF Plugin A pluginRequires Plugin B AND Plugin B pluginRequires Plugin A (directly or transitively)
businessRules.action: THEN configuration MUST be rejected as circular plugin dependency

changeControl.changeNotes: v4.0.0: Added Skill, Plugin, ProcessPath, PathStep, and PathLink entities. Added 14 new relationships (invokesSkill, agentProvidesSkill, skillDependsOn, providesSkill, pluginRequires, pluginExtendsDesignSystem, hasPathStep, hasPathLink, stepRefersToProcess, stepInvokesSkill, linkFrom, linkTo, pathImplementsPattern, pathScopedToInstance). Added 9 new business rules (BR-009 through BR-017). Deprecated AIAgent.capabilities free-text property in favour of agentProvidesSkill relationship. ProcessPath is a directed graph with no fixed start — entry determined by PathStep.entryPoint flag and runtime context. Mirrors EMC-ONT ComposedGraphSpec pattern for process composition.

versionHistory.notes: Upgraded to OAA v6.1.0: converted 14 object cardinalities to string notation, updated namespace URIs to v6.
Upgraded to OAA v5.0.0 JSON-LD compliance

What is the role and purpose of ProcessMetric within this ontology?
What is the role and purpose of AIAgent within this ontology?
What is the role and purpose of ProcessInstance within this ontology?
What is the role and purpose of Hypothesis within this ontology?
What is the role and purpose of ValueChain within this ontology?

What skills can be invoked from a given process phase, and which agents or plugins provide them?
What plugins are available at a given cascade tier, and what skills do they expose?
What are the possible process paths from a given entry point, and what binding strengths govern each transition?
